vulnerability.hypothesis: In IsObjectEscaped (line 284), the PostWriteBarrier case unconditionally breaks without checking which operand the scalar-replaced object is. MPostWriteBarrier has two operands: object (index 0) and value (index 1). If the newly-allocated object is used as the VALUE operand (index 1) of a PostWriteBarrier—meaning it's being stored INTO another object—the escape analysis incorrectly considers it non-escaped. The object gets scalar-replaced (eliminated), but another object holds a reference to where it should have been. This leads to use-after-free or type confusion when the other object's slot is accessed expecting the original object but finding garbage/reused memory. Compare with StoreFixedSlot (line 277) which correctly checks `def->indexOf(*i) == 0` to ensure the object is only the container, not the value being stored.
